baseline.read: Human-visible guardrails block promotion: 5/5 groups visible, arrival continuity 0.3, magic-appearance risk 0.7, spacing 0, bunching risk 1.

bestSelection.read: Human-visible guardrails block promotion: 5/5 groups visible, arrival continuity 0.71, magic-appearance risk 0.29, spacing 0.19, bunching risk 0.81.

bestReadability.read: Human-visible guardrails block promotion: 5/5 groups visible, arrival continuity 0.93, magic-appearance risk 0.07, spacing 0.37, bunching risk 0.75.

leastBunched.read: Human-visible guardrails block promotion: 5/5 groups visible, arrival continuity 0.93, magic-appearance risk 0.07, spacing 0.35, bunching risk 0.69.

bestDeconflict.read: Human-visible guardrails block promotion: 5/5 groups visible, arrival continuity 0.93, magic-appearance risk 0.07, spacing 0.35, bunching risk 0.69.

bestRouteAware.read: Human-visible guardrails block promotion: 5/5 groups visible, arrival continuity 0.93, magic-appearance risk 0.07, spacing 0.32, bunching risk 0.75.

summary.read: The stronger direct deconflict family now beats the earlier readability/timing family on bunching, but it remains far from the promotion gate. Route-aware offsets reduce magic-appearance risk and are now a useful grammar seam, but they did not beat deconflict-only on bunching in this pass, so they should remain measured rather than promoted.

nextPrimitive.read: Move entire challenge groups as authored routes before applying per-lane/object separation. This should reduce inter-group overlap while preserving the feeling that aliens arrive in coherent Galaga-like waves.
